mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is stable, because easing without inflation pressure is interpreted as policy support rather than a recession warning.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 25 bps over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events do not show positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if returns are negative on average despite declining FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE is a contraction signal that tightens household income expectations and raises recession odds, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the following week. The effect should be negative when unemployment is moving up faster than payroll growth can offset it.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 pp from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns on average, or if the sign is positive in a majority of triggered instances over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strengthening RSAFS indicates improving retail demand and near-term revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is validated when sales growth is broad enough to imply consumer demand resilience rather than one-off price effects.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline positive 20d SPX returns, or if the sign is consistently negative during periods of improving retail sales.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and profit-cycle support, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and raises S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should be positive when output growth is not accompanied by a sharp rise in unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO uptrends fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp HOUST decline signals weakening housing demand and tighter future consumer spending, which weighs on equity returns over the next month. The signal is negative because housing weakness transmits into construction, durable goods, and broader risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the average forward return remains positive after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS accelerates while UNRATE is flat or falling, labor demand is broadening without an obvious slack shock, which supports household income and equity returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if employment gains are offset by a simultaneous unemployment increase.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by at least 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll growth episodes do not lead to positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal loses power whenever UNRATE is not improving.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS rate above prior-cycle neutral levels compresses valuation multiples and raises the discount rate applied to future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not falling, because policy remains restrictive rather than pre-emptive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and rising over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if tightening triggers fail to produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if returns remain positive even when FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL is not decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising GDPC1 and strengthening RSAFS indicates synchronized real and nominal demand growth, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is not active when GDP is flat because retail strength alone would then be interpreted as price inflation rather than volume growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior-quarter or prior-3-month levels
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1/RSAFS improvement does not precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal is absent when both series improve together.
